title: ferray-ufunc: complex transcendentals missing (complex exp, log, sin, cos, sqrt, power)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's umath provides transcendental functions for complex types: np.exp(complex_arr), np.log(complex_arr), np.sin(complex_arr), etc. These use separate C loops for complex types (loops_unary_complex.dispatch.c.src). ferray-ufunc's transcendentals (sin, cos, exp, log, etc.) all require T: Float, which excludes Complex<f32> and Complex<f64>. The complex module only has real/imag/conj/angle/abs. Add complex transcendentals — the num_complex crate already provides Complex::sin(), Complex::exp(), etc.
